coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of what can go wrong when language models are deployed, from harmful outputs to misuse
Elementary knowledge of alignment as making model behaviour match human intent
Ability to identify clearly unsafe responses in model outputs under supervision
Basic knowledge of how bias shows up in model completions
Ability to run predefined safety test cases against a model
Understanding of fairness and why representational harms matter
Documentation of safety issues with clear reproduction steps
Elementary knowledge of responsible AI principles and guidelines
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with model evaluation harnesses and benchmark suites
Basic knowledge of adversarial prompting and simple jailbreak attempts
Elementary understanding of interpretability as inspecting why a model responds
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Identifies clearly unsafe issues in model outputs
Is building foundational AI safety skills

coreCompetencies: Ability to evaluate a model against a safety rubric covering harmful and disallowed content
Implementation of bias detection across demographic slices of prompts
Practical knowledge of adversarial robustness and how prompts break safeguards
Ability to red team a language model to surface jailbreaks and unsafe behaviour
Understanding of safety benchmarks and what each one measures
Capability to propose mitigations such as prompt hardening and output filtering
Knowledge of alignment techniques like instruction tuning and preference feedback at a conceptual level
Ability to write clear safety evaluation reports
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of interpretability methods for inspecting model internals
Ability to build small harnesses for repeatable safety testing
Understanding of emerging AI regulations and their requirements
indicators: Evaluates model safety with supervision
Implements and tests mitigations
Documents risks with reproducible evidence

coreCompetencies: Design of safety evaluation suites that cover a defined threat model

Understanding of red teaming methodologies for frontier models
indicators: Leads safety evaluations for a model or feature

Mentors others on AI safety practice
